finding: The workflow could generate a structurally valid provenance statement and trusted-builder decision without producing an explicit verifier-status manifest or final fail-closed acceptance artifact. The new wiring preserves that distinction and keeps unsigned evidence rejected.

covered: signature remains explicitly not_verified even when local checks pass
untrusted builder identity is rejected independently
verification.committed_workflow_status: No GitHub Actions result observed for the workflow wiring commit. Repository commit status only reported unrelated Vercel build-rate-limit failures.

privacy_boundary: No secret values are emitted. Only public deployment URLs, reason codes, digests, source identity, workflow identity, and boolean/status outcomes are recorded.
trust_limit: The workflow does not yet execute a cryptographic signature verifier. Therefore signatureVerification.status is deliberately not_verified and final accepted remains false.
falsification_route: A missing cryptographic verification step maps to verified.
A proof-byte digest mismatch maps to match.
A lookalike builder or source identity maps to trusted.
The workflow accepts evidence while signatureVerification.status is not_verified.
The committed contract tests fail in CI or a clean checkout.
external_blocker: Configure CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID and CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN in the cloudflare-research GitHub environment and dispatch the deployment workflow.
